text.tspan: What is the effect of ancestral

tspan.tspan: Morphological disparity — the variation in form among organisms — is a key
metric in macroevolutionary studies. However, many taxa in phylogenetic
trees are internal nodes without observed traits, requiring ancestral state
estimation (ASE) to estimate their likely morphologies.
A major challenge is that ASE is inherently uncertain, particularly under
complex models of trait evolution or when fossil sampling is sparse. Despite
this, most disparity analyses treat ancestral states as fixed point estimates or
apply simple threshold rules — potentially propagating bias downstream.
